source.title: Recent Developments in the Habit Hypothesis of OCD and Compulsive Disorders

confidence_rationale: Frames OCD compulsion as a CONSEQUENCE of a goal-directed (model-based) control deficit -- the override that would license stopping a no-longer-warranted action is impaired -- not a primary habit strengthening. Supports failure-to-disengage as a distinct mechanistic pathology located in the model-evaluation/override pathway. Mechanism is action-selection-level, not goal-bank-level, hence held at 0.72.
failure_signatures: The agent keeps executing a stereotyped sequence (checking/washing) triggered by a cue even though its own outcome knowledge marks the action as unnecessary -- slips of action against devalued outcomes.
Behaviour is rigid and stimulus-bound: it cannot be flexibly halted by a change in goal value because the goal-directed controller that would halt it is impaired.
Subjective recognition that the action is senseless coexists with inability to desist -- a dissociation between explicit goal knowledge and behavioural control.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Compulsions in OCD arise from an imbalance between goal-directed and habitual control in which enhanced habit expression is a CONSEQUENCE of a deficit in the goal-directed (model-based) control system -- specifically a failure to construct/consult an internal action-outcome model that would license re-evaluating and halting a no-longer-useful action.
mapping.ree_translation: Supports ARC-078 C3: failure-to-disengage is a distinct pathology located in the model-evaluation/override pathway -- the agent cannot bring goal knowledge to bear to stop. For REE, the abandon mechanism should target the model-evaluation/override pathway (the thing that would license stopping), NOT add a habit-suppression term or merely strengthen persistence.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The goal-directed/habit dichotomy is a model of action selection (model-based vs model-free RL); the review itself flags human habit assays are weak/contested. The defensible reading is 'the model that would justify abandoning the action is absent/not consulted', not literally 'habits override goals'. Review, not a single experiment.
mapping.source_context: Mechanistic review synthesising cross-species, neuroimaging, and computational evidence in OCD / compulsive disorders.

confidence_components.notes: Authoritative review by the field's leading author (high source quality). Mapping fidelity moderate: failure-as-missing-override maps well; action-selection level vs goal-bank level adds a mapping qualifier. Transfer risk moderate.
